I was gifted a six month subscription and was beyond excited for it. Having previously read reviews about how frustrating it was to have to wait for the next box; I waited until I had the full set before sitting down to "play" with a friend. To say I was disappointed would be an understatement.

The items sent in each box were generally a handful of printouts and a sprinkling of plastic garbage from a bulk retailer. The story often felt forced to bend around and include whatever random favor they threw in the box that month.

The clues in some instances required huge bodies of knowledge the average person doesn't have, or giant leaps to reach the expected conclusion.

Some examples:

1. A 1.5 inch piece of plastic thread taped to the back of an astronomy themed postcard. Apparently I was too know the postcard depicted Orpheus in constellation and the plastic tubing was also to indicate a link to Orpheus and his musical talents. What was the significance of Orpheus? Simply to let you know to continue looking forward. Irrelevant to the game's overall solution.

2. a postcard showing the name of a city. This was included to confirm the writer's previous claims of being from that city. How that confirms anything is beyond me.

3. A printout of some email correspondence between two people. This was too prompt you to email one of the parties yourself so that you could receive an automated response with more information. Because when I play a game that advertises "everything you need to solve the case is in the box" I automatically think about how I'm going to have to email fictional characters as part of the game play.

While playing it also became clear that things were missing. Some boxes lacked all the items they were supposed to come with. I ended up finding that out when I was finally able to locate lists of what each box should contain online. Furthermore, through research online, though this was supposed to be a six box story, it actually required more than six boxes. I didn't have two or three of the necessary boxes to solve the case.

As this was a gift I had no idea how insanely expensive it was until I had to start looking online to figure out what was missing from my packages. For the price, this is a total rip off. I would not recommend it for yourself or for a gift. Spend that money on a murderer mystery dinner theater, or an escape room. I guarantee you'll be happier with either of those options than with this garbage scam.

Honestly, the most satisfaction I got from this game was when I was able to dump it straight in the trash and clear off my table.
top_profane.title: Don't waste your time or your money

top_rant.text:
How to Be a Redhead is a great beauty subscription box with a unique specialty— it’s for redheads. It’s available as a regular monthly subscription (for $25) or deluxe subscription (for $65 which arrives quarterly) both are well worth it and both are packed with goodies that are suitable for redheads!

For years I wanted to buy one of those monthly beauty subscription boxes, but I never did because I knew from experience that any time I’d buy an all inclusive selection of makeup (skin products, or eyeshadow palettes etc…) inevitably I end up getting annoyed and frustrated that almost everything in it is suited to “average” skin tones, which (compared to most redheads) that ends up being about 3-to-4 shades too dark— and therefore half of the pre-selected items would be useless— and that’s if I’m lucky! It usually ended up being more like 80% useless products that are all wrong for a redhead’s skin.

I understand the redhead population is relatively small (it’s estimated at only 1%–2% of the world’s population) but that said, it’s still a lot of people and as consumers, a redhead’s money is worth every bit as much as a blonde’s or a brunette’s, so it annoyed me how everything was catered to being either blonde or brunette and redheads (along with other groups) were all relegated to one “miscellaneous” category. Understanding this is one thing but I still wished I could easily find suitable products.

After years of feeling left out, I stumbled upon #H2BARbox. It’s been a godsend.

Each month your $25/ monthly subscription box is supposed to deliver a selection of redhead-friendly products to your door, said to be valued at many times more expensive than that $25/month subscription amount— and FTR, I do believe this to be true. I’m generally a skeptical person so I usually go online and check the individual prices myself just to make sure their claims hold up and to make sure I’m really getting my money’s worth.

Now, to be clear— it’s not always makeup, and it’s not always skincare. On average, the breakdown is APPROXIMATELY 70%-30%, the majority of them being hair and skincare products. Another complaint I read was the products are not always JUST for redheads.

NB: #H2BAR never claimed they curate products that are for “REDHEADS ONLY”, it’s just that some people apparently read into their description too much and then were disappointed the products were I guess “too versatile”? Since it’s estimated that Redheads only make up about 1%–2% of the earth’s population, it shouldn’t be surprising that makeup and beauty product manufacturers are not rushing to gear entire product lines towards such a small, niche market. Of course only SOME products will be for “redheads only”… but ALL will be “redhead friendly”, and that’s what was missing from other beauty box subscriptions.

The only real complaint that I have is that the perceived value of boxes is sometimes inconsistent. That is to say, some months the amount you get *feels* like you are really getting a great value and you can easily see how the sum of the products would retail for well over $250… but other months it doesn’t seem that way and it feels like you’re getting less. They would do well to even it out.

So to sum up, it sometimes feels lopsided. One month you might get a tube of sunblock, a fabulous full size Paul Mitchell shampoo and conditioner, a cute little makeup bag or brush, a nice moisturizer or a face serum, a pretty lipgloss in a redhead friendly shade, a mascara or eyebrow pencil (also in redhead friendly shades) and maybe a sample or two (usually of a higher-end, new &/or more expensive product, like toner or face serum) and on those months, you couldn’t be happier with your subscription. Those months you can easily see the value and you are thrilled with what you’re getting for $25…

but then other months— you’re less than thrilled. Some shipments feel too heavy on the samples. Samples are great too,and they’re appreciated in a beauty subscription box— especially when it’s a luxury brand—but add too many samples and it starts to feel (and look) cheap. Plus, it gets annoying.

I always put samples aside for “travel” but then they end up being forgotten (or passed over in favor of bringing something else I’ve already tried and I KNOW works for me and that I’m sure I like) samples often get lost or expire and then have to be discarded, after they’ve withered away at the bottom of my beauty stash.

Another issue I have with the excessive sample sizes is that the writing on the packages is always minuscule (or non-existent) and you have to save the little postcard that comes along with each shipment (which details the items they included that month, describes what it does, and/or its ingredients) and it inevitably gets lost or thrown away. In my experience, you pretty much have to use them right away— but, depending on what the samples are (e.g. sunblock or deep conditioning masque) you don’t necessarily need or WANT to use them right away, but I always find if I don’t use the samples right away, they just fall by the wayside. If I can’t identify the contents of the sample and it’s been sitting in my drawer for months. If I’m not sure of the contents, I usually just end up throwing them out.

Although I have checked H2BAR’s claims (I’ve googled each and every product) and I found that YES, technically the products WOULD retail for at least $250 and each box DOES work out to be roughly the same value from month-to-month (dollar-wise… the items always retail for CONSIDERABLY more than the $25/ month subscription price) but some months it doesn’t SEEM like you’re getting an equivalent amount compared to previous months. One month you’re delighted with your #H2BARbox haul… but then other times you feel little bit disappointed and the amount feels a little thin, by comparison.

For example:
Claim: to (usually) send full-sized products

Truth: Yes. Mostly this is the case— USUALLY, *but* occasionally (especially when they include something like a boutique brand, or higher-and serum, for example or some other prohibitively expensive item no one could possibly reasonably expect to receive a full-sized container of that item (along with several other products) inside their $25/monthly subscription box… they just give you a department store style sample. Sometimes there’s an online coupon code for10%-15% off that product (in addition, or sometimes INSTEAD of an actual sample) for said item. Those are annoying and truthfully (because you’d have to shell out additional money for that item to even get the discount) I don’t factor those into the overall value of the #H2BARbox or even appreciate getting those at all. It also doesn’t help that I usually don’t even know anything about the brand, or if I even like the product they’re giving me the coupon for, plus they ARE asking you to drive to a store (or order online and pay shipping) and you’d have to shell out more cash. These days, if you want me traipsing around a mall or shopping center, then you’re going to have to do better than that. Usually the discount they offering is ONLY about 10% or 15% off. That’s probably the manufacture’s doing, but still… #H2BAR IS including these coupons on occasion and they make it feel off-brand— very tacky.

That’s all well and good, but looking at the contents on those “thin months”, I can safely say that if that paltry LOOKING box (even though the cash value is probably equal to or greater than the others) can be a detriment. Last month’s box was a little heavy on the samples for my taste, and honestly I would have preferred it if they’d combined the value of those trial sizes and just thrown in one full size product instead (not even necessarily that same item or brand) just a full sized SOMETHING, rather than tons of trial size it’s preferable to the alternative, which is getting the same number of fluid ounces as the full size, only in sample/ trial-size form. If that month had been my very first foray into #H2BARbox, then I might have been one of those people griping online about it, and my first shipment would have been my last. My only complaint is about the lack of consistency there was in *perceived value* of boxes from one month, to the next. First impressions are everything and some months H2BAR makes a better impression than it does on others.

If I had received a sample-heavy box my first (or 2nd) month, I probably would have just canceled my subscription right then and there too. The majority of negative reviews I have read though are from people who are unhappy because of the of people who use hair color, well news flash: there are people who are natural redheads and who also use hair color, sometimes to touch up gray hair, sometimes just for fun.

When you love a product, you’re happy. When you’re not-so-in-love with what they sent, you want to know you at least got your money’s worth. Most people are not going to have the patience to google the price of each item like I did. People just eyeball it. They size it up, and rely on *perceived value*. If customers are happy because they *think* they’re getting a good deal, then they usually ARE getting a good deal.

Overall though, I still think the #H2BARbox is fantastic. It’s a great gift for a redhead (or for anyone with similar pale or rosy skin tone) there is nothing else like this— not that I’ve ever seen.

As a company though I do think they would do well to make each monthly shipment more consistent in its *perceived value* (vs. its actual/cash value, “costs a lot” doesn’t always = “looks expensive”)… because judging by the (relatively few) negative Amazon reviews, that seemed to be the most repetitive theme.

The other complaints were about the brands. There are no “cheap” brands. Some reviewer was upset because she’d never heard of a particular brand of skincare product, so she decided they must have thrown it in there because it must be “cheap”— meanwhile it was a luxe boutique brand and she wasn’t familiar with it because the entire line was brand new, she’s obviously a bargain shopper and it’s not sold in drugstores.

If you’re someone who’s brand loyal, who has a narrow scope of “faves” And you just want to use the same products over and over again, then H2BAR (and in all likelihood, also any other beauty subscription box) is probably just not for you.

It’s like handing a friend $25 and saying “You know I’m a redhead and you know what kinds of beauty products I usually buy, Here’s $25— surprise me!”

If that sounds like a fun idea or something interesting you’d be curious to try, then you’ll probably love #H2BARbox too.

If you are someone who’s open to trying new beauty and skincare products, if you’re someone who loves little surprises and who enjoys discovering and trying new brands— AND you also happen to be a redhead, then you’ll probably love it too.
top_rant.title: Best beauty subscription for redheads

top_rant.text:
VMP offers yearly subscription for a cheaper price if you buy a year at a time. I also get a email notification from them and I can either skip the copy or get a previous copy to sell as they sell for a lot compared with most record clubs. It’s more expensive, but they make the covers which are very thick and all of the pressings are exclusive and 180g pressings. Some are even 45rpm remastered. I know many people are going to say they don’t like VMP or it’s too expensive. But I like knowing what I am getting and have plenty of time to exchange it, or if I don’t want a duplicate or something else, I will get a month’s credit.

The one thing that Amazon has FINALLY made an improvement with is their packaging for vinyl. The old method was absolutely terrible and I never purchased vinyl from Amazon because of this issue. I recently purchased two Phish LP on LP copies and discovered that they finally updated their packaging procedure!

Almost every album I would order, would arrive with either bent edges or corners, or worse, large seam splits or both! I can’t say it’s the post office every time either. I returned a copy of Bob Mould - Workbook 25 because it had about 2 inches of the spine missing from poor packaging. Another issue I have had with Amazon and vinyl pressings is that in the past, I ordered two Mobile Fidelity Sound Labs pressings and they kept saying it wasn’t in stock. I finally bought the two pressings from Music Direct, the owner of the MoFi label now, and canceled the Amazon copies. I don’t know why they didn’t have them in stock as they where not exactly low numbered repressings like some of the newer ones. However Amazon charged me for the crappy mp3 that they sent, instead of removing them. I don’t do mp3 format! Yes that sounds like a snobbish remark, but it’s a crude format! It’s why I have owned 4 Astell&Kern players over the years and I buy downloads that are in 24Bit/192 kHz or DSD. MQA is an amazing sounding format as well and I own several MQA CDs that I have ordered from Japan! You need a computer and special DAC to unfold the true sound of these releases! I have some great downloads that are 24Bit /96kHz as well. Amazon still charged me $9.99 each for the two albums they could have gotten and it was for a service I don’t even use! I don’t understand why they didn’t have the copies in stock in the first place because everyone else online had them in stock!

My last complaint about buying vinyl pressings from Amazon is that third party sellers are not listing their used vinyl pressings in the correct place. Yes, I know this Vinyl of the Month subscription is different, but their used Vinyl and CDs is a complete mess! I have sent back several that were listed as a certain pressings only to get a different pressing! Some of these albums have upwards of 200+ pressings. Yet they let third party sellers list them just anywhere! I purchased a copy of Queen - The Game that was supposed to be a MFSL copy and NM. It had a photo of the MFSL album . I even sent an email to the seller saying that if it wasn’t an MFSL issue, don’t send it! Well I got a terrible US pressing that was dirty and had severe ring wear. I tried to contact the seller and he was gone with my $75.00.

I haven’t purchased any Vinyl or CDs or SACDs from the third party sellers in a long time. I just got feed up with buying items that where listed incorrectly or the sellers idea of NM is much different than what the average person would consider NM, even to the people who have no clue about grading vinyl.

I’m going to post a question before I decide on weather or not this is a Record of the Month club I would want to be part of or is it not something I would like. So far the titles listed are copies I already have a few different pressings of. I’m also not a huge fan of picture disc, as they sound terrible for the most part. I have two copies of Pink Floyd - The Wall, one is a Japanese pressing and the other is a MFSL pressing. I have 5 copies of The Clash - London Calling. A earlier Japanese pressing, a red unofficial version, a couple of U.K. pressings and I bought a Clear Vinyl 50th Anniversary release that I have not played or opened as it is worth over $450.00+ on Discogs. I got it for about $70.00.

I’m also a little bit put off by customers saying that they got a different copy for the month than others got. That never happens with VMP! So I will have to see what response I get to my question about these albums that are being sent out before I join another vinyl club!

top_rant.text:
I received the 25 snack box for the first time and was not impressed, the box came 1/4 filled, and the other 3/4 was empty space, what a ripoff for $40 per month. Everything was super tiny, I could fit several items in the palm of my hand. The snacks included 3 little bags of pretzels, various kinds of jerky, lots of energy bars, and two little bags of random chips. These things seem like stuff they couldn't sell at a grocery store and they just packed it up in box to resell for unsuspecting customers. I appreciate that the snacks are healthy but I think I can definitely get more worth of my $40 buying it from a supermarket, personally. Maybe if the cost of the subscription box was halved I would continue my subscription, but at the current price, it's best to look elsewhere for snack boxes.

I forgot to cancel my subscription so I got a second box, still full of the same little tiny pretzels, energy bars, and jerky. At least this time I got a small bag of cookies...It feels like this company really loves their pretzels, I have so many pretzels now instead of a variety of chips or crisps, how disappointing. To inform how little you're actually getting, I had combined both snack boxes together and there is still space for probably a third box of snacks. If the original box included twice the amount of snacks it had, it would be a better deal, and I would feel like it was worth getting, despite the lack in variety of snacks. As for the taste, I tried about seven snacks and they were OK, but not anything impressive, since they are basically your everyday snacks you could buy from a grocery in mini form.

Update:
SnackNation offered to provide some additional snacks for no cost for me to try due to me being unhappy with my previous boxes, and I accepted. They shipped me two mini boxes, which were completely stuffed to the brim (unlike my giant box of mostly empty air), but I can't say if this would be true for subscribers for their newer boxes, so I would reserve my judgement on this until I try a full sized box from them.

The new snacks they gave me were surprisingly bigger than what they had given me in the past, and again, I do not know if these changes will be reflected in their new boxes, but they had promised me they were changing their curations for future boxes, so I hope this is true for the sake of their subscribers. They included a variety of snacks which I hadn't seen before, and I would say I was happier with this selection that I received for free vs. the selection I had gotten when I paid for the subscription, but this might be due to the changes they were making to their boxes as they had mentioned to me. The snacks are still on the smaller side, but not so much as before, where you felt you got cheated somehow by the tininess of everything. I would say the new snacks are appropriately snack-sized for human beings to consume now, instead of for tiny fairies. But they also included some duplicate snacks between the two mini boxes I received, which could be either a bonus or a con, depending on whether the snack in question tastes good or not.

For example, they put the same bag of RIND snack in both boxes, and unfortunately, I have to say this snack is one of the most unpleasant things I have ever placed in my mouth. I've only gotten through half of the first bag and I will question my sanity if I try to attempt to finish the second one. I appreciate the RIND company for trying to come up with healthy snacks, but their versions of dried fruits is absolutely disgusting. The fruits included were dried pineapple, dried kiwi, and dried orange with the peel on. On the back of the snack bag, it encourages you to eat the peel since it contains a lot of nutrients, even more than the fleshy part of the fruit. So I tried to heed their advice and put the whole dried orange in my mouth, peel and all, and chewed it for three seconds and had to spit it back out. It was the most bitter thing I had ever tasted in my life, and I've had tried many bitter things before, but not to this extent. The dried kiwi was like dried extremely sour ??? covered with a very light coating of sweetness which was wholly unpleasant. Only the dried pineapple was passable, but honestly, it tasted like a vampire sucked all the juice out, chewed it a couple times, then spat it into the snack bag for us mere humans to finish.

However, the other snacks I have tried have been great so far, and I will post further updates as I go through my snack boxes. I am still undecided whether to restart my subscription, but perhaps I will give them another chance in the future if my finances allow. For now, I would bump my rating to 2.5 stars.
top_rant.title: Boxes full of miniature snacks for fairies, not people!

top_rant.text:
I was so excited for this. I am currently toward the end of writing something I have been working on for three years so I opened this up like it was going to spring forth some golden light and writing fairies were going to come out handing me pens and stickers and little trinkets that I had never thought of.

But I got some hot chocolate. I can’t eat chocolate or sugar. So that was a waste.

I got some really ugly fabric thumbtacks from the couture collection. I like the idea. But these bright orange and black, crayola blue with halloween orange triangles all on a tiny pin for me to post on a wall of my work is never going to happen. They belong in a remainder pile somewhere. Oh wait, maybe that’s where they found them!

And then I got a coaster that looks like a record player but says, when you read it around in a circle “Replaying my thoughts and plots- Scribbler”. Not a bad idea but the coaster is made from a weird material. It feels awkward. I wouldn’t trust my hot drinks on this thing,

Then there was a small booklet that gave some help on writing. I quite liked that. It had some very good ideas.

There is an a button that says, “Ask Me About My Novel.” I have not yet become that pretentious. Nor do I ever plan too. And if I did, it wouldn’t be with a teal and bright yellow pin.

And lastly a book and a nametag with the writers name. It isn’t even written in the book. Quite odd.

If I was shown this beforehand and asked how much I would pay for it, my answer would be nothing. No thank you. I am not sure whether to try again or if this is truly what this has to offer. If so, I am horribly disappointed and would tell you to save your money.

That was my review for November and then lo and behold, I get another box for December even though I was sure I cancelled it. When I contacted the seller, they said they couldn’t accept returns because there is food in the box. So that is why you are getting that random child sized raise box this month!

And a fairly unattractive duo tone tote bag saying “Between the pages is a lovely place to be” with an open book and some black trees on it. Behind that there is a golden mountain in the background with lines flashing out like when kids draw a sun on pictures. Looks more like a warning than a welcoming picture. Again you can make these at zazzle or many places online. (worth $3.95 or less)

Then there is a twenty one page notebook about cliffhangers including an interview with the book writer and nine pages of dos and donts. For example, DO have compelling characters. DON’T drone on and on with description and a paragraph describing why. Felt a little pedantic to me but was probably just the format of the do’s and dont’s in capital letters and the simplicity of the message. But maybe it will be right on for some people. Who knows? The interview is fairly decent but can that information be found easily online by searching writing and cliffhangers basically.

The one pamphlet I did like was the “Inside Look” into the publishing process where they shared Charlie N. Holmberg’s story pitch for “Spellbreaker.”. That was interesting and helpful to read. All two and a half pages of it.

So, all in all I paid $41.50 ($39 plus tax) for what I could have gotten for $10.95 minus the three pages about on story pitch. But if you don’t have Kindle Unlimited like me and have to pay full price for the book, it would cost you $20.94. It’s reasonable to assume you could get the paperback for cheaper than $10.99 though.

Anyway, beyond just the cost of this was the excitement and wonder as had for this that was lost. I bought the November box as a birthday present for myself for my 47th birthday. It was beyond disappointing and I am always very unhappy when the sellers won’t even try to work with you especially when they send articles I am allergic too.

I have taken a picture so you can see for yourself. I want you to know what you are getting yourself into before you buy. Maybe you will love this! And then that will be great. Good on you.

But if it isn’t what you expected like me and if spending almost fifty dollars is a lot of money for you and something you try to spend cautiously then this box is not what you want to spend it on. Many blessings everyone!

top_rant.text:
Got the Deadpool box. It was meh. Between the cool Pop and keychain I would have paid 30 but it wasn't a "deal". Skipped Venom. Not a big fan. Marvel 10 year!!! This could be cool. Maybe a bunch of small stuff spanning all the movies. Maybe a large exclusive avengers Pop! Nope.
Ironman 2 with Ironman fighting Whiplash as the big pop. A pez and a couple of dorbz. If this had been $10 I wouldn't have bought it.
10 years of Marvel and you picked the big Pop from the Movie that's consistently in most fans bottom 5. 10 years and that's your go to. And a hulk pez from Ragnorak. Meh. And two dorbz from Ragnorak. Meh and you already covered that with the pez.
Never again. It's a piddling amount of stuff for a loot box but also horribly curated.
The Funko Pop does look cool. It's well made and it was one of Ironman's cooler suits but come on. 10 years to pull from.

Subscription officially cancelled and no box will entice me back.

Edit** Three things.
One I should talk about the overall subscription. They say 4 to 7 items. Of the three boxes they’ve had two with 4 and one with 5.... Also t-shirt not guaranteed despite giving t-shirt size with subscription. Okay. Of three boxes only one has had a shirt and if I’m reading it right the X-Men box won’t have a shirt. So one of four. So while this review mostly hit the ten year box the overall value would also get a one.

Two. In case anyone from Amazon or Funko is reading here’s how you might fix this. Send everyone who got the ten year box a shirt with every character in the MCU to date in funko form. Fighting. Holding hands. Just standing there. Whatever. Don’t forget Stan Lee. Then for the X-Men box you need shirt with a team of X-Men. If you have a box like X-Men or 10 year you have to have a shirt. Stress ball. Then an X-Men logo keychain. Then a side character something. Nightcrawler. Jubilee. A character maybe someone wouldn’t normally buy but are happy to see. And 7 items in this box. You’re buying back some credit.

Third. What the 10 year box should have been. The above mentioned shirt. That covers 10 years. The Pop. Iron man in the Mark 1 armor with no helmet. Or Mark 1 half off, the desert scene after the fight. Or Mark 1 with flames coming out of flamethrower. First superhero moment in MCU. There are other options. A Stan Lee pop. But the Mark 1 would have been good. Something Avengers. Keychain. Decal. Check. Something Guardians of the Galaxy. Then a few random whatevers. A Teseract stress ball or paperweitht. Infinity Gauntlet something. Black panther. Empty keychain labeled Ant-Man. Etc. So if anyone from Amazon or Funko! Is reading this here you go.

2nd edit.
Saw the Funko Marvel subscription was in danger of being below three stars. All of a sudden a sprinkling of 5 star reviews appear. Just enough to keep it at 3 stars... Wish I could give a negative 5 stars for that cheap play on Funko or Amazon's part.

3rd edit
Just Sam the X-Men box. Yeah. Glad I cancelled. I honestly don’t know how they even value the contents at $30. $15 pop, $5 socks, $1 pencil topper, $3 notebook, and candy for a few bucks. If they’re claiming these things cost more than that retail then wow. Consumers complained and they doubled down with less value and worse crap.

Ok, I’ve had over bcA year of dealing with this subscription company-and I’d like to share a. Review from two viewpoints-the subscription department end & the product end of it.

Subscription department: I’ve had a subscription for over a year now, SOMETIMES TWO! How? Bc at one point they somehow started another one with my card information-( I think it was bc I tried to prepay for 3 months at a time.) One month I even got 3 BAGS!!! I had referred my mom to the club & at the time the reward they were offering was a free bag,-instead if adding a month on my pre-paid account-they just sent an additional bag for that month! So I got 3 bags of lip stuff I ended up not using!! Since it was a prepaid gift card, I wasn’t too freaked about canceling it & became amused at what exactly they were going to try to keep pulling with it as long as they were allowed. When they kept trying to charge me for a 3 month subscription I DID try to find where & how to cancel it, but it’s not obvious. I finally decided to just send it to their customer care department and it wasn’t ever acknowledged. As a subscription company I feel their customer service is non-existent. Unless you are subscribing you won’t easily find anyone to help you.

PRODUCT REVIEW: see subscription review! No I know I need to be more clear-there were a few good products that I got from them? But overall it’s a complete miss. I’m not the first to say this! The colors are either brown or bright pink or orange, and I keep getting repeats sometimes as soon as the month after! Plus it’s NOT 5 FULL SIZE products, it may be one with a full chapstick and some deluxe samples...or the lip pencil they send doesn’t match the lip color that they sent...and they love orange! I’m not kidding I thought it was a colored pencil it was so weirdly bright orange!! Orange! Not coral. I wish I had kept all of them I would have posted a pic of them...if I ever come across one that I didn’t just toss, I’ll post a pic.
Now you might say it’s bc I’m not hip on today’s colors, but I have 2 teen daughters,17 & 16, and uh nope! I can’t push it off on them either!
Oh and the flimsy pouches they send the product in? Boo!! (I guess I’m spoiled by Ipsy!)

So overall-please don't get mixed up with this company! Don’t even just try them for a month bc it’s a total waste of money. Instead just go out and buy one nice tube of your fave shade-AND USE IT! You will be 20 times ahead of all of us that wasted $$$$. Plus really-how many shades do you wear?

The average gal barely gets time to throw her face on, before she runs out the door to start her day anyways! So if you get the time to put on actual lipstick, you might as well wear what you really love right?!

****By the way, if you want to have a fun experience ithe a beauty subscription, get the glam bag or plus from IPSY...AS OF TODAY DO NOT GET THE Ultimate Glam Bag from them, they just don’t have their act together with it yet? But I’ve been a subscriber from almost their beginning & they are always there if there’s a problem, and I get two regular bags & a plus every month. Plus they have super discounted specials on different products everyday. Love them.

top_rant.text:
First of all, these shirts are all of good quality. They're soft, well-made and comfortable. After being washed a few times, the decals are still completely intact with no fading or peeling. No complaints there.

As far as the designs go, it's a really mixed bag. I've received seven of them so far. Some I love, and some I hate. Here's how I would rate them in the order in which they arrived:

Shirt #1: Painted Beholder - Rating: 5/5
This is my favorite of the bunch so far. The image is of very high quality, mostly red, green and white on a black shirt. The beholder looks fearsome and awesome, and it seems to be about to do battle with an elven adventurer. Absolutely love it.

Shirt #2: Dagger and Dice - Rating: 2/5
I wasn't very impressed with this one. The colors are bright to the point of being garish. The design itself is a bit shoddy and looks more like a rub-on tattoo from a gumball machine than something professional. I don't much care for white shirts, maybe it would have looked better on black?

Shirt #3: Drizzt Do'Urden - Rating: 4/5
This one's pretty cool. It features Drizzt in a stoic pose, with the outlines of Guenhwyvar, Icingdeath and Twinkle around him. The colors are fairly simplistic, mostly purple, black, orange and yellow. It's on a navy background, which compliments it well. Very nice overall.

Shirt #4: Choose Your Monster - Rating: 3/5
This one is a bit silly, but it's cute. It features classic Dungeons and Dragons monsters set up in a grid to resemble the start screen of a video game. The monsters featured are an Owlbear, a Lich, a Beholder, a Mind Flayer, a Red Dragon, a Displacer Beast, a Mimic, a Tarrasque and a Gelatinous Cube. The shirt itself is a nice asphalt gray. The only reason I didn't rate it higher was because it looks a little too busy, it might have been better if the size of the decal was a little smaller. I do like it though.

Shirt #5: Never Split The Party - Rating: 1/5
I HATE this one. It's sloppy, ugly and poorly designed. It features a faded map, the outline of some of RPG dice, and the slogan "never split the party". It feels like someone just slapped some ideas together with no concern about how they'd look when combined. The decal looks a bit fuzzy, as though it were enlarged from an image with poor resolution. It's also on a white shirt, which as I mentioned earlier, I don't really care for. This is the only one so far that I've returned for a refund. Amusingly enough, Amazon was kind enough to say that they'd give me the refund without my even having to send the shirt back. I guess they don't want it either.

Shirt #6: Acererak Card - Rating: 5/5
This shirt is so cool! Devoted Dungeons and Dragons players know exactly who Acererak is. Anyone who has faced the notorious demilich in his Tomb of Horrors would probably love to have this fun shirt to remember that classic adventure. It's a black, white and red design on a black shirt. Acererak is even rolling a pair of d20s as though he wants to join in the roleplaying fun. I love this one, it's among of the nicest of the bunch so far.

Shirt #7: Know Your Monster - Rating: 4/5
Another awesome shirt! The color of this one is a nice silver, which is far preferable to the white ones. It features five iconic monsters; Remorhaz, Beholder, Owlbear, Gnoll and Red Dragon. There's a snippet of detail about each monster and a black and white image of them that resembles a sketch an adventurer might jot down in their journal. There's a bit of color behind each monster to add some extra flair. It's a very nice design.

Overall, I am happy with the T-Shirt subscription. It's fun surprise to see what shirts are going to arrive each month. Amazon has been fair about returns/refund for the one I didn't like. It's not a bad deal that way - get a new shirt each month, keep the ones you like and return the ones you don't. :)

I look forward to my shirt every month, and I do recommend this subscription for Dungeons & Dragons fans.
top_rant.title: It's A Mixed Bag, But Still Fun

top_rant.text:
I have just received my first box and was prepared to be somewhat disappointed after reading quite a few reviews...
The delivery took a long time and I was super excited to finally receive my first bag of goodies.
The bag itself is super cute but not of fantastic quality, the zipper is pretty flimsy.
I received 4 full sized products (the product description on amazon said 4-5 products per month) but unlike advertised only 3 of them were lip products - the accompanying card said the company decided to switch things up with a “Monthly Mixup” - not sure I’m super thrilled about that.
The first thing I noticed is that there was no description given for the products I received or the manufacturers of those products.
I actually had to google the items to determine which one wasn’t the lip item! Since I subscribed to try out new products and companies I’m unfamiliar with I’d very much appreciate some pointers along with the products!
Now to the products themselves:
1. cougar lipgloss in cocoa - the website promises 24hrs of matte lips in “the perfect nude brown” - not a color I’d have picked but I think I’ll be able to use it on occasion
2. Cargo gel lipstick in Sicily - a bright, bold red. It’s a pretty color but certainly not fit for everyday use for me. Again not a color I’d have bought for myself.
3. Appeal gel liner in deep taupe - it’s a dark brown rather than what I’d expect from a taupe and as a lipliner it will be useless to me. The company’s website claims it can be used both as lip liner or eye liner so I’ll try it as an eyeliner and might actually get some use out of it. The color works with neither the lipstick nor lipgloss. It would have been really nice had they been coordinated!
4. Bellapierre shimmer roll in a deep bronze color - google let me know that this is the non lip product and that it is a roll on highlighter. I do not use highlighter and even if i did I don’t think this would be the color for me... I might try it a shimmer layer over eyeshadow or lipstick and see whether that works.

I’ll try another month or two to get a better idea of whether this just wasn’t the box for me or whether none of the boxes are for me before I’ll decide whether or not to continue this subscription.
I expected to not only get colors or products I’d have bought for myself - that’s part of the fun with a non personalized subscription like this.
But if the selection is so far out of the neutral, should fit most types and styles spectrum every month then I’ll not keep collecting a growing stack of useless products.

Update with November box:
Again 4, not 5 products:
1) peony lip scrub - it’s a very small tube but on the company’s website that’s the actual size offered
2) lipmonthly liquid lipstick - I think, the packaging only states it’s 02 of whatever it is, looks like a nude enough color to maybe work for me
3) lord&berry lip crayon in a statement color I couldn’t find on their website - what I could find there however is, that the crayon apparently requires a particular sharpener that can be bought on their website
4) a single eyeshadow in a very adventurous color.
I could have forgiven this, after all I don’t expect colors handpicked for me BUT it is a single eyeshadow that’s meant to be clicked into a palette and is going to be awkward to use on its own since it literally has no edges to nicely hold on to and the lid slides off since it’s not meant to stay on once it’s been put into the palette.

So essentially 2 out of the 4 products received would require additional expenditure to be used as intended by the manufacturers.

I’ve decided to not wait for a 3rd box and have cancelled my subscription.
top_rant.title: I have cancelled after receiving my second box - updated review

top_rant.text:
UPDATE November, 2019:
I think it's time to put my money into a different subscription. Each month, there are fewer and fewer full and "deluxe sample" size products. Those that are full size aren't worth much money. There's no point of me putting my money into so many samples.

OVERVIEW:
I just received my first Cocotique box. I was a little nervous, as I'm new to subscription boxes, but I'm actually quite satisfied with the products. Most of them are full-size, and others are large enough to get quite a few uses out of. The month's box (the actual cardboard box the products are in) is absolutely beautiful! Included is a card with extensive information about all of the products, including prices and where to find them should you decide to purchase them. I absolutely love that they included this because I can easily find the products if I want to order them, and I can see the value of the products versus the cost of the box ($25/month).

PRODUCTS:
With that being said, the value of the products in the box is well worth the cost of the subscription (in this box, at least). The price of the products come up to about double the cost of the box (approximately $47). So, I'm happy with the value. You get more than 2 or 3 items, and they're pretty large.

CUSTOMER SERVICE CONTACT:
One item (eyeshadow) was broken (still sealed). I emailed them on a Saturday and they responded within 2 or 3 hours to apologize and let me know that a replacement was on the way. I greatly appreciate the quick and polite customer service.

SHIPPING:
Shipping was EXTRA fast, but...things are not safely secured in the box. I'm not sure if this is how the eyeshadow box was broken or not, but the products definitely need to be better secured. The products are packaged with pretty crinkled paper, but not enough to hold the products in place. Some products are heavier than others, which may cause some issues in some boxes. This is reason for the 4-star review. All of the products were strewn throughout the box in an unorganized way. Unfortunately, mail carriers don't (and sometimes can't) always take the time to ensure that things aren't thrown around during shipping, so more secure packaging may make things a little better.

BILLING:
I have to be honest and say that the way they do their billing is a bit...odd to me. They bill on your anniversary date, even if the box isn't due to be shipped for weeks. I signed up in early to mid-March, and I just received my first box, the April box. This, of course, depends when you sign up, but when you initially sign up determines if you receive your first box for the CURRENT month or the month after. I was just now charged for next month's box, even though I just received this month's (April) box today. Again, I'm fairly new to subscription boxes and the other ones that I'm subscribed to charge on your anniversary and send your box when you're charged. YES, this is stated in multiple places on Cocotique's website and other places, so I was aware of it, but again, it's just a bit odd to me because it seems like you're waiting for a very long time for something you've paid for. But, that's the way they work and I'll more than happily deal with it to see what they have in store.

BOTTOM LINE:
I will be continuing my subscription. I'm fairly new to the makeup world, so being able to try a wide variety of products (again, these products are large, so you can do a lot more than "try" them) without having to buy all of them one-by-one comes in handy. It saves both money and time. I've definitely told my friends about this subscription box, and I'm excited to see what will be in future boxes. I think I'll be ordering some of the past boxes, also.

As time goes by and I receive more boxes, I'll update my review.
top_rant.title: Was great...not so much now.

top_rant.text:
After seeing a video online for this service, I decided to give it a try. I just received my first box, but it was underwhelming. This is what I received:

Standard PC AC cable
Molex to SATA power adapter cable
80GB SATA 1 hard drive
PCI based modem card
Excelius retractable cleaning brush

The parts were packaged well and included a handwritten manifest of the parts that were given.

I realize it is a mystery box and what you get will be different every month and different than what someone else gets, but the claim that every box contains $55 or more of parts is not accurate based on my first box. At best I’d say $15 tops. The only item of any value is the hard drive, but at only 80GB and SATA 1 it is only good for use with older systems and laptops...fine for Windows XP or 7. That’s really about a $10 used part based on prices on the bay. The rest of the parts I got were “meh”. The cleaning brush is nice but I’m sure it costed them maybe $1 each to have a bulk order of them purchased with their company name put on them. I’ll see what comes in the next box a month from now as I want to see if it is going to be crap again or if it will have some better parts worth more than what I paid. So far, not even close. I like the thrill of a mystery box, but it better live up to claim that there’s $55 or more in value. I’ll update my review either way to help those of you wondering if this is a good deal or not.

UPDATE 2020-01-27: I reported the problem to the seller, and I was contacted almost immediately after that. The seller gave me a partial refund. He explained that I should have received much better items than I did and that for my next box he would personally ensure I receive items that meet what is promised in the product description. I bumped up one star for great customer service. I will review again after I received my next box.

UPDATE 2020-02-27: The box I received was better in February. There was a functional 500GB SATA hard drive, a Chromebook (parts only), an Excelius wallet tool (very cool), and HP rechargeable laptop battery, and an HP motherboard. The motherboard has some bent cpu socket pins, but I might be able to repair it. I'm not sure what I can do with the battery and Chromebook, but at least the LCD of the Chromebook looks like it may be working. I will report-out again in March on the quality of that box.

UPDATE 2022-03-29: Boxes got really good for the most part over a year. Then the box I received Feb 2022 had nothing but junk...literally maybe $10 worth of parts. There was no list of parts as there normally is, and the box did not have the Excelius label on it. Then a week ago my subscription was canceled. I did not initiate canceling it either. The service was great IMO this past year...not sure what the heck is going on now, but I'm not happy now.
top_rant.title: Initial impression wasn't good, but the seller is making things right

Edited to add on July 7, 2019:
Subscription cancelled and I am reducing my 4 star rating to one star because I went to their website and looked at the cost of each of the chews sold separately and the total cost would be $15.72 plus flat rate shipping $7, so total cost would be $22.72. The first box was a good price and being able to try a variety of their chews was nice, but seeing how they could be bought separately for less than the $29.99 it would cost each month, the subscription box is not at all a good value.

Original review:
They advertise 7 different types of chews per box, and 7-15 total chews per box. I am now assuming looking at the card insert and photo in the product description that boxes for large dogs cost more because some of the chews are larger and it is assumed they’ll consume more chews each month so they get more per box.

I ordered the small size box for dogs under 20 lbs, and did receive 7 different types of treats (all 7 of the varieties shown for small dogs on the card the sent, see pic), and a total of 9 treats (see pics... they came in 3 bags... two of the bags had 3 different types of chews and the third bag contained the 2 large pig skins).

They appear to be high quality chews and my 2 dogs went nuts at the smell of the box, they knew it was something for them as I opened the box and tried to take the first pic! Because we received 2 pig skins I gave them each one and because they were so large I planned on taking them away after a few minutes so they could enjoy them over several days (I usually do with that large chews)... and about 3 minutes later they had each already devoured half of their chew! Apparently they love those chews so much they are whining about having them taken away (sorry, pups... but those are too large for you to gobble up in a matter of minutes)! I should mention my dogs are both about 2 years old and 12-15 lbs... one’s a Scottie/Yorkie, the other a chi/dox... both with long narrow mouths, and neither have been aggressive chewers... but they went nuts for those pig skins like I’ve never seen before!

That said... I am cancelling our subscription because 5 of the kinds of chews we only received one chew (pig skins and beef esophagus strap 2 each)... but with 2 dogs I need to give them each the same thing or they’ll want whatever the other has. Also, the chews are rather large for our dogs’ small mouths, the puff braid is supposed to be small size but it’s so large they’d have a hard time chewing it (they’d probably try... but possibly it’d harm a small mouth... I may give that chew away to a friend’s larger dog). Yes, larger chews can last several days, but I don’t want them eating a large chew all at once and then when I take their chews away I always feel guilty.

I possibly would not be cancelling our subscription if there were double the number of chews that were half the sizes for smaller dogs. 5 stars for the flavor of the pig skins (only thing they’ve tried so far, will lower the rating if they really dislike a different chew), but I’d rate 3 1/2 stars overall for my dogs as I don’t feel the larger sizes of the most of the chews are appropriate for a dog under 20 lbs.

Update: I just received the April 2021 box and it too is AMAZING!!!! This is my 3rd or 4th box and I think they get even better with every new box!!!!!! I added a pic for this months. It's the red one.

By far the best subscription box I have ever subscribed to. Every single item is super cute, great quality and functional. I thought I had every item of Friends merch they made until I got this box. Most of the items are not items of Friends stuff you see everywhere. For example, the chick and the duck salt and pepper shakers! Definitely a first for me and they are adorable!
I have included a picture of everything that came in the Fall 2020 box. You can choose your size for clothing and the sizes are unisex. I couldn't even throw away the box it came in. I had a hard time throwing away all of the smaller boxes the merch came in, inside the big box!
If you're hesitant, go for it! You can always cancel your subscription if you don't like your first box. Also, since each item comes individually packaged, give the items you don't like away as gifts to other Friends fans.

We'll be back!!!!! Thank you Bark Box for helping out our local shelter and for a great product!

top_five_star_obscene.text: I recently received my 4th box and wasn't disappointed. With every box, I evaluate should I cancel or keep it but there are always 2-4 things I find I can't live without and reorder. When I evaluate the top ticket item ROI, it's a great value for the money. What I don't use/need I gift to my step-son/sons-in-law that appreciate and look forward to them. My only criticizism to GQ and Amazon, be aware of regional/seasonal limitations. Box 2 arrived the end of May when our average temperature was 105 degrees and the box sat in a mail truck for 6 hours. Items melted or the caps melted shut and went immediately into the trash.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Money well spent

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Been a subscriber for 3 months now. Got some things I love and use everyday. Got some things that went to the shop in the garage to be used in the future. We moved to a new house about 3 months ago and my tools are spread all over between the garage, the shop, the shed, and the house. Around midnight I needed a flat head to swap out the swivel sling on my Grandfather’s Remington 700 30-06 the other night. Couldn’t find what I needed without waking up my wife. Remembered I had the Tactica M250 in the shop and got the job done. No one was hurt except a few beers.

Not been disappointed yet. The BattlBox subscriber community on Facebook is friendly, apolitical, and funny as hell. The owners/operators interact frequently and graciously accept feedback.

No complaints on my end.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Good to go - Satisfied - But When Will The Zombies Come?
